learning_outcomes.description: Analyse the complexities of founding, investing, and growing a business by applying innovation and entrepreneurship theory.
Develop evidence-based solutions to real-world business model innovation challenges, individually or as a team.
Communicate entrepreneurial insights and ideas to generate stakeholder buy-in and build diverse partnerships.
Reflect on the development of your entrepreneurial mindset, self-efficacy, and social capital.
Conduct primary research through experimentation and/or stakeholder engagement to validate and refine proposed solutions.

assessments.assessment_title: Project or business case proposal presentation
assessments.due_date:
Week 3 Tue - Week 4 Tue

Presentations will be scheduled in class time from 5PM during weeks 3 and 4.
assessments.task_description:
Individual or team (if WIL is based on working with own startup team) presentation of 10 minutes followed by Q&A.

  1. Identify and analyse an innovation and/or entrepreneurship issue relevant to your placement: a startup, incubator, accelerator, or business industry partner.
  2. The assessment piece must demonstrate thoughtful application of theory to innovation and entrepreneurial, or more specifically, venture creation and growth practice.
  3. The main research objective/s or question/s must be related to finding a solution to a practical problem or business model innovation issue encountered during the WIL, placement, or own venture that students want to investigate further by applying existing theories and research.
  4. You will therefore do a brief presentation to outline and explain your intended topic, objectives, methodology and/or approach as well as the expected outcomes of your major assignment piece (Report or business case). This will vary in accordance with the nature of the placement that you are undertaking. 

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: In person and slide upload to Blackboard. Please submit your presentation slides on Blackboard BEFORE you present.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: You will sit a single secure in-person interview, framed as a reflective discussion with a potential employer or investor, structured in two parts. In the first part, you will speak to an unscripted set of questions about what you learnt during your placement. In the second part, the interviewer presents a short, unseen case or scenario drawn from the innovation and entrepreneurship domain, and you must apply your placement learning to it live, reasoning aloud about what you would do and why. The discussion remains conversational with the interviewer probing both the reflection and the transfer.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires you to document and analyse the work you completed during your Work Integrated Learning (WIL) placement.

Assessment 3 builds directly on your previous work:

  • Assessment 1 outlined your problem, research questions, and proposed methodology
  • Assessment 2 captured your reflections on your WIL experience.
  • Assessment 3 presents what you actually accomplished, the value you created, and what you learned

Your report should be suitable for both academic assessment and your partner startup/organisation (if applicable).

Your report should address:

  1. Project Context: Briefly revisit your Assessment 1 proposal—the problem you addressed, research questions, and any adjustments made based on feedback
  2. Execution & Findings: Document what you actually did—activities, data collection, analysis, and discoveries. Include specific evidence (numbers, quotes, examples)
  3. Deliverables & Recommendations: Present tangible outputs and evidence-based recommendations for your partner organisation or venture
  4. Theory-Practice Reflection: Critically reflect on how entrepreneurship methodologies influenced your work. Connect to insights from your reflective interviews where relevant

Required elements:

  • APA-style references (separate reference list)
  • Professional presentation/format suitable for external stakeholders
  • Tables, figures, or charts may be included to support the discussion
  • Optional appendices for key artefacts (e.g., interview protocols, Business Model Canvas)
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
